headline: Ux Ui Designer | Design System | Sr. Ui Designer
about:
Mi nombre es Juan Carlos Neciosup, con 5 años de experiencia como UI Designer y 2 años como UX Designer. Me especializo en trabajar con metodologías ágiles como Design Thinking y Scrum, lo que me permite crear soluciones efectivas y adaptables.

Mantenerme al día con las últimas tecnologías, métodos y técnicas es esencial para mí. Disfruto colaborar estrechamente con desarrolladores, stakeholders y product owners, y he comprobado que una comunicación efectiva mejora significativamente la calidad y el tiempo de entrega de los proyectos.
Mi enfoque se centra en alinear los objetivos de negocio con un diseño que genere un impacto real. Tomo decisiones basadas en datos para garantizar mejoras continuas y resultados óptimos. 

Además, me apasiona desarrollar e implementar sistemas de diseño robustos y escalables. Un sistema de diseño cohesivo no solo garantiza la coherencia de la interfaz, sino que también optimiza el flujo de trabajo del equipo. Contribuir a la creación y evolución de estos sistemas es algo que me motiva profundamente.

My soft skills:
La empatía es una de mis principales fortalezas. Me esfuerzo por comprender las perspectivas y experiencias de los demás, lo que me permite conectar de manera auténtica y significativa. Valoro el aprendizaje continuo y siempre estoy en busca de oportunidades para crecer y perfeccionar mis habilidades. Creo firmemente en la honestidad y la transparencia, y me aseguro de que la confianza sea el pilar de todas mis relaciones.

experience.title: Especialista de Diseño UX/UI
location: Santo Domingo de Guzmán, Distrito Nacional, República Dominicana
experience.description:
As a user experience and user interface designer, my role comprises:
 
-Empathizing and understanding the user and the customer, in context. Conducting interviews, empathy maps, Bechmarking, creating experience maps, customer journey maps, user persona, etc. 

-Define the structure by making a content inventory, organizing the information architecture, and building user flows.

-Create sketches and ideas, on paper and/or digital but in a simple way, to focus this stage on the structure and operation rather than on the design. Studying how it connects one screen to another and analyzing the whole flow of a user from the moment he starts to use the product until he concludes the desired action.

-Creating Lo-Fi wireframes in Figma or Adobe XD, to start thinking about more concrete structural aspects, such as spacing, margins, paddings, selecting typography and typographic scale, among others.

-Start the Atomic Design process where I first start by creating components and smaller parts, and their variants, adding the color palette (making sure that the contrast between colors complies with accessibility standards), and defining all assets, images, and illustrations. Then I go on to form the more complex pieces.

-To create High-Fidelity frames applying Auto-Layout, where you can see the finalized design and visual style.

Then I proceed to create prototypes, adding micro-interactions and animations to emulate the experience as close as possible to the final product. 

-With this, I carry out a process of testing, analysis, and iteration to constantly improve the product. To make an usable, equitable, enjoyable, and useful product.

I have a degree as Industrial Design Engineer that lead me to build a strong foundation in Human-Centered Design methodologies (even though I didn't know it back then). Life works in mysterious ways, and after some trials and errors, I met UX design, and my life has never been the same. 

UX changed the way I think and how I build relationships with others. I understood that we exist in a world designed by humans that is not really for humans, and I love to have the chance to jump in and challenge the way things are now, with a strong foundation of inclusion and empathy.
